events.title: Cloud Foundations Bootcamp
events.summary: A hands-on day covering core cloud concepts and your first deployment.
events.description: Start from zero and leave with a deployed project. This full-day bootcamp walks through compute, storage, and networking fundamentals, followed by a guided lab where every participant ships a small app to the cloud. Laptops and a GitHub account are all you need — the Student Builder Group team handles the rest, from setup to troubleshooting.

events.title: Build Night: Team Hack Sprint
events.summary: Form a team, pick a problem, and ship a working prototype in one night.
events.description: Build Night is a five-hour sprint where small teams pick a real campus problem and prototype a solution before the clock runs out. Mentors from the core team rotate through every table, snacks are provided, and the night closes with two-minute demos judged by peers. No experience required — just bring curiosity and a laptop.

events.title: Speaker Session: Life at Scale
events.summary: An industry engineer on what breaks first when systems grow up.
events.description: We're bringing in a practicing cloud engineer to talk candidly about what actually happens when student projects turn into production systems — the outages, the re-architecture, and the lessons that never make it into tutorials. The session closes with an open Q&A, and the speaker stays back for informal conversation over refreshments.

events.title: Git & GitHub for Beginners
events.summary: Version control from first commit to your first pull request.
events.description: A gentle, practical introduction to Git and GitHub for students who've never used version control before. We cover commits, branches, and pull requests, then pair everyone up for a mock collaboration exercise so the workflow actually sticks. By the end, every attendee has opened and merged a real pull request.

events.title: Resume & Portfolio Clinic
events.summary: One-on-one reviews to get your resume and GitHub interview-ready.
events.description: Bring your resume, your portfolio link, or just your project list — core team members and invited alumni will sit with you for a focused fifteen-minute review. Expect direct feedback on structure, project framing, and what recruiters actually look for on a student profile.

events.title: Open Source Contribution Day
events.summary: Make your first real pull request to a live open-source project.
events.description: A full day dedicated to open source, from picking a beginner-friendly issue to opening your first pull request against a real repository. Mentors help with setup, git workflow, and code review, and the day wraps with a shared board of everyone's merged (or in-review) contributions.
